name: ollama
description: Get up and running with Kimi-K2.6, GLM-5.1, MiniMax, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, Qwen, Gemma and other models.

latestRelease.notes:
## What's Changed

* Enabled flash attention on older NVIDIA GPUs (compute capability 6.x)
* iGPU can now offload vision models with padding to fit available memory
* Fixed structured output for thinking models when thinking is disabled
* Hardened GGUF model creation
* `ollama launch` for Claude Code now disables telemetry by default
* Fixed loading models on paths with non-UTF-8 characters
* Updated the MLX and llama.cpp engines

## New Contributors
* @kevinpark1217 made their first

name: vllm
description: A high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs

latestRelease.notes:
# vLLM v0.24.0 Release Notes

## Highlights

This release features 571 commits from 256 contributors (77 new)!

* **MiniMax-M3**: Added support for the new **MiniMax-M3** model (#45381), with a fast follow-on of BF16/FP8 indexer via MSA (#45892), MXFP4 support (#45896), FP8 sparse GQA (#45744), and extensive AMD/ROCm tuning — mxfp8 MoE/linear on gfx950 (#45725), fp8_per_channel for bf16 weights on MI300X (#45854), FP8 KV-cache fix (#45720), and packed-modules mapping (#45794). latestRelease.notes:
## Verify Docker Image Signature

All LiteLLM Docker images are signed with [cosign](https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/overview/). Every release is signed with the same key introduced in [commit `0112e53`](https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/commit/0112e53046018d726492c814b3644b7d376029d0).

**Verify using the pinned commit hash (recommended):**

A commit hash is cryptographically immutable, so this is the strongest way to ensure you are using the original signing key:

```bash
cosign verify \
  -

* release(langgraph): 1.2.9 (#8316)
* fix: updateState metadata/counters for delta channel (#8315)
